title: Convert TSV (tab-separated values) file to CSV using Python stdlib
description: Converts tab-separated values (TSV) to comma-separated values (CSV) using Python's standard library csv module.
explanation: The command reads TSV data from stdin, parses it with csv.reader using tab delimiter, and writes it to stdout using csv.writer with default comma delimiter. It preserves quoting and escaping rules.
author: Marcos de Carvalho

examples.command: cat data.tsv | python3 -c "import sys,csv; csv.writer(sys.stdout).writerows(csv.reader(sys.stdin, delimiter='\t'))" | head -20

notes: Handles quoted fields with tabs correctly
Works with any TSV dialect compatible with Python's csv module
warnings: Large files may consume memory as all rows are read into list
Input must be valid TSV format
